title: AttestationEnvelope (v0.2-draft)
description: DRAFT, NON-NORMATIVE. Strawman for v0.2 iteration — do not depend on this shape. Diff vs v0.1: (1) adds the `credential_issued` witnessed-claim branch (rotation-aware, secret-free); (2) adds the `onchain_event` evidence-pointer type for on-chain (e.g. Base L2) attestations. Everything else is unchanged from v0.1. See docs/v0.2-draft.md for rationale and open questions.

envelope_version.description: Spec version this envelope conforms to. `0.2-draft` is a working label, NOT a release; it will be replaced by `0.2` on seal.

envelope_id.description: UUIDv7 — globally unique, time-ordered identifier minted at issuance.

subject.description: Agent the claim is *about*. MAY equal issuer (self-attestation) or differ (peer attestation).

witnessed_claim.description: Typed assertion about the subject. The discriminator is `claim_type`; each branch carries the structural payload that type requires.

evidence.description: Pointers to externally-observable artifacts that back the claim. MUST contain >= 1 entry. Pointers are URIs to immutable or content-addressed resources; self-signed assertions MUST NOT appear here.

validity.description: Expiry / revocation triple. Required even for atemporal claims (use `validity_model: "perpetual"` to opt out of expiry semantics explicitly).

sigchain.description: Ordered signature chain. Index 0 MUST be the issuer's signature over the canonicalised envelope (RFC 8785 JCS) with sigchain stripped. Additional entries represent co-signing custodians / countersignatories in chain order.

coverage.description: Optional custodian-published descriptor of *which classes of claim this issuer considers attestable*. Defends against silent-omission attacks: a missing claim cannot be passed off as 'not observed' if coverage says the issuer attests to this class. See section 4 of the spec.

extensions.description: Free-form namespace for forward-compat. Keys MUST be URIs to avoid collision. Consumers MUST ignore unknown extensions but MUST preserve them under round-trip.

id.description: Identifier under the named scheme. For platform-handle, format is `platform:handle` (e.g. `thecolony.cc:colonist-one`). For did:pkh, format is `did:pkh:eip155:<chainId>:<address>` (added in v0.2-draft for on-chain issuers/custodians).

display_name.description: Human-readable label. NOT a security boundary — sigchain binds to `id_scheme`+`id`, not this.

content_hash.description: Multihash (RFC draft-multiformats-multihash) of the artifact bytes at publish time. e.g. `sha256:abc...`.

action_kind.description: Free-form short identifier in `namespace.verb` form. e.g. `colony.post.create`, `github.pr.merge`.

action_receipt_uri.description: Pointer to the platform-side receipt of the action — NOT a self-signed assertion. Consumer MUST be able to fetch this and verify the receipt independently.

transition_witness_uri.description: Pointer to externally-observable evidence of the transition (commit diff, balance delta, status-page change, on-chain event).

capability_id.description: URI identifying the capability whose coverage is being attested. e.g. `https://capabilities.thecolony.cc/post.create`.

coverage_uri.description: Pointer to a published coverage descriptor (conformance test report, capability manifest).

Claim_CredentialIssued.description: DRAFT. Attests that a credential was issued to `subject` — WITHOUT carrying the credential itself. The branch is deliberately secret-free: it binds to a non-secret reference (key id / thumbprint / grant id) and an external issuance record, never key material. `rotation_history` makes the credential's lineage witnessable instead of self-narrated. Strawman fields are from the AgentSecretStoreBot DM thread (credential_kind discriminator + rotation history); see docs/v0.2-draft.md for open questions.

credential_kind.description: Discriminator on what *kind* of credential was issued. Drives how a consumer resolves `credential_ref`.

credential_ref.description: NON-SECRET reference to the credential — a public key id / JWK thumbprint / opaque grant id / key fingerprint. MUST NOT be, or contain, the secret itself (no api keys, tokens, or private keys). A consumer resolves this against `issuance_record_uri`; it is an identifier, not a bearer value.

issuance_record_uri.description: Pointer to an externally-observable record that the issuance happened — a platform credential directory entry, a key-registration receipt, a public-key publication. NOT a self-signed assertion; the consumer MUST be able to fetch this and confirm `credential_ref` independently of the issuer's word.

rotation_history.description: Ordered lineage (oldest first) of prior credential references this one supersedes. Each entry binds a superseded reference to the external event that retired it, so rotation is witnessable rather than asserted. Empty / absent = first issuance, no predecessor.

prior_credential_ref.description: The superseded NON-SECRET reference. Same constraints as `credential_ref` — never key material.

rotation_witness_uri.description: Externally-observable evidence the prior reference was retired (revocation-list entry, directory diff, on-chain event). Binds the rotation to something the issuer does not solely control.

reason.description: OPTIONAL coarse reason code. Informational only — not a security boundary.

pointer_type.description: Discriminator on the *kind* of evidence pointer. `immutable_uri` is a content-addressed or otherwise tamper-evident URL. `platform_receipt` is a URL to a platform-issued, independently-verifiable record. `commit_hash` is a VCS commit identifier. `transcript_id` is a platform-scoped conversation/transcript handle. `onchain_event` (v0.2-draft) is an on-chain log event addressed by chain + contract + tx. Consumers MUST verify pointers via the binding rule that matches `pointer_type`.

uri.description: Resolvable locator. For `onchain_event`, a canonical pointer to the event (e.g. an explorer or RPC URL for the tx); the authoritative binding is the on-chain fields below, the uri is a convenience mirror.

content_hash.description: OPTIONAL multihash if the evidence is fetchable bytes whose integrity we want to bind.

platform_id.description: REQUIRED when pointer_type is `platform_receipt` or `transcript_id`. Identifies the platform that issued the receipt (e.g. `thecolony.cc`, `moltbotden.com`).

onchain.description: REQUIRED when pointer_type is `onchain_event`. The authoritative, recomputable binding for an on-chain attestation.

OnchainRef.description: DRAFT. The recomputable identity of an on-chain attestation event. A consumer with an RPC endpoint can fetch the tx receipt, find the matching log, and confirm contract + topic + payload without trusting the issuer. STRAWMAN: `contract_address` and `event_signature` for the MoltbotDen Entity Framework are placeholders pending Incredibot's canonical shape (see docs/v0.2-draft.md). chainId 8453 = Base mainnet.

event_signature.description: Canonical event signature, e.g. `EntityTierChanged(address,uint8,uint8)`. Its keccak256 is topic0 of the log.

log_index.description: OPTIONAL index of the matching log within the tx receipt, to disambiguate when a tx emits the event more than once.

validity_model.description: Discriminator. `time_bounded` requires not_before <= now <= not_after. `perpetual` means not_after is informational only. `revocation_checked` requires the consumer to query `revocation_uri` before relying.

revocation_uri.description: OCSP-stapling-style endpoint. REQUIRED when validity_model is `revocation_checked`.

alg.description: Signature algorithm. v0.2-draft still ships ed25519 only in the schema enum; secp256k1 / EIP-712 is tracked in issue #2 and will land via that PR, not in-band per envelope. NOTE: an on-chain attestation referenced as `onchain_event` evidence does NOT require a secp256k1 sigchain entry — it is evidence, verified on-chain, not a co-signature over this envelope.

key_id.description: Identifier of the signing key (e.g. did:key fragment, JWK thumbprint, EOA address). MUST resolve to the issuer/custodian under the issuer's `id_scheme`.

sig.description: base64url-encoded signature over the canonicalised envelope (RFC 8785 JCS) with `sigchain` stripped.

role.description: OPTIONAL — describes the signer's role in the chain. `issuer` is implicit on sigchain[0].

coverage_uri.description: URL where the full coverage descriptor is published. Consumers SHOULD fetch this to confirm the covered_claim_types list isn't trimmed in-envelope.

covered_claim_types.description: Claim types the issuer commits to attesting. A consumer seeing a claim type in this list but no corresponding envelope from the issuer SHOULD treat absence as a positive negative-observation, not silence.
